what_is.title: What is PHI OS
what_is.body: PHI OS is a Chrome extension that solves the memory gap between Claude.ai sessions. It automatically saves your work at the end of each conversation and restores the important context when you start a new session. No more re-explaining the same things to AI.

how_commit.title: How Saving Works
how_commit.body: Your session is automatically saved when: ① you close the tab ② you switch to another tab ③ 20 conversation turns have passed ④ you are idle for 5 minutes. The saved content includes 5 types: new facts, decisions, pending TODOs, tensions/doubts, and next session hook.

how_restore.title: How Restore Works
how_restore.body: When you open claude.ai/new, PHI OS automatically injects the key context from your last session into the input field. This allows you to start a new Claude chat with your previous context already loaded. Up to 3,000 characters of context are restored.

mode_switch.title: Mode Switching
mode_switch.body: PHI OS has two operating modes: ① Standalone Mode (default): operates without any server connection. No internet required. ② MoCKA Connected Mode: when a local MoCKA server (localhost:5000) is running, advanced recording features are automatically enabled.

lang_switch.title: Language Switching
lang_switch.body: Use the language selector in the top right of the screen to switch between 日本語, English, 中文, 한국어, and Español. Your preference is saved for future sessions.

panel_switch.title: Panel Mode Switching
panel_switch.body: You can switch between popup and side panel display from the toolbar icon's right-click menu or the settings screen. Side panel is recommended for wide-screen work.

items.a: Open claude.ai/new. Restore only runs on new chat pages, not existing ones.

items.q: Content doesn't seem to be saved
items.a: Check that PHI OS is enabled at chrome://extensions. If storage is full, use the 'Delete Old Data' button in settings.
Reload the extension via the update button in chrome://extensions.
Start the MoCKA server with MoCKA-START.bat so localhost:5000 is responsive.
